Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a lens product with its specifications and attributes.

sensorSize.description: Sensor size supported by the lens.

efl.description: Effective focal length of the lens.

maxImageCircle.description: Maximum image circle of the lens.

fovDiagonal.description: Field of view (diagonal) of the lens.

fovHorizontal.description: Field of view (horizontal) of the lens.

fovVertical.description: Field of view (vertical) of the lens.

ttl.description: Total track length of the lens.

chiefRayAngle.description: Chief ray angle of the lens.

pdfUrl.description: URL to the product datasheet PDF.

ExcelImport.title: ExcelImport
ExcelImport.description: Represents an import of product data from an Excel file.

id.description: Unique identifier for the excel import record.

filename.description: Name of the imported Excel file.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the Excel file was uploaded.

recordCount.description: Number of product records imported from the Excel file.

SupplierLens.title: SupplierLens
SupplierLens.description: Represents a lens product from a unified supplier database.

sensorSize.description: Sensor size supported by the lens.

efl.description: Effective focal length of the lens.

maxImageCircle.description: Maximum image circle of the lens.

fovDiagonal.description: Field of view (diagonal) of the lens.

fovHorizontal.description: Field of view (horizontal) of the lens.

fovVertical.description: Field of view (vertical) of the lens.

ttl.description: Total track length of the lens.

chiefRayAngle.description: Chief ray angle of the lens.

pdfUrl.description: URL to the product datasheet PDF.

definition.description: Stores product details. Includes all fields as defined in the Product schema.
Stores metadata about Excel imports, including the filename, upload date, and record count.

params.name: excelImportId
params.description: Unique identifier for the excel import record.

definition.description: Stores lens product details, typically extracted from PDFs.
Stores lens data from various suppliers.

params.name: supplierLensId
params.description: Unique identifier for the supplier lens.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to efficiently manage product data imported from Excel files, support searching and filtering, and provide a foundation for future AI-driven insights.  The core principle is authorization independence achieved through structural segregation.  The `products` collection stores product details. A separate `excel_imports` collection stores metadata about each Excel import. This segregation supports different access control needs, as access to import metadata might be restricted to admin users or the user who uploaded the file. 

QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters) are addressed by the segregation of data into collections with homogeneous security postures. Listing products does not require filtering based on ownership or roles; therefore, the rules can efficiently grant or deny access to entire collections or documents.

Future considerations:

*   For the AI Product Insights feature, consider creating a separate collection (e.g., `/product_insights/{productId}`) to store the generated summaries. This would allow for efficient retrieval and management of insights.
*   If user-specific preferences or saved searches are implemented, these could be stored under the `/users/{userId}/preferences` path.
